steps.title: Use the no-real-solutions boundary
steps.content: For $-x^2+bx-676=0$, the discriminant is $$b^2-4(-1)(-676)=b^2-2704$$ A quadratic has no real solutions only when this value is negative. That gives $$b^2-2704\lt 0$$ so $$b^2\lt 2704$$ This is faster than trying many values because the discriminant tells you exactly where the graph stops crossing the x-axis.

steps.title: Find the integer endpoint
steps.content: Since $2704=52^2$, the inequality is $b^2\lt 52^2$. The question says $b$ is a positive integer, so $b\lt 52$. The endpoint $52$ is not allowed because it would make the discriminant exactly zero, giving one real solution instead of no real solutions. The greatest possible positive integer is therefore $51$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: At $b=51$, the discriminant is negative, so there are no real solutions. At $b=52$, the discriminant is $0$, which gives one real solution, so it is too large. The answer is 51.
